title: Fix Calibration Toggle and resetAudioContext Bug
description:
# Fix Calibration Toggle and resetAudioContext Bug

## đź“ť User Story / Description
The Calibration Signal UI toggle fails to bind to `toggleCalibrationMode`, rendering the checkbox unresponsive. Furthermore, the `setInterval` drift correction function attempts to invoke `window.resetAudioContext()` which is no longer attached to the global `window` object due to ES Module migration.

## âś… Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `resetAudioContext` is imported correctly and invoked without `(window as any)`.
- [ ] `testSignalToggle` binds correctly to `toggleCalibrationMode` in `src/main.ts`.

## đź› ď¸Ź Technical Notes
- Update `src/main.ts` line 28 to import `toggleCalibrationMode` and `resetAudioContext`.
- Add event listener in `src/main.ts` for `#testSignalToggle`.
- Modify line 818 to use direct reference `resetAudioContext()`.

## đź›ˇď¸Ź Rigor & Verification Boundary
- [ ] UI checkbox successfully triggers calibration.
- [ ] No `TypeError` in console log during interval loops.

- **Quality Debt Exemptions:**
  - `complexity_limit: false`
  - `doc_drift: false`
  - `dry_candidate: false`

### đź“ť Git Commit Details
```
Fix Calibration Toggle and resetAudioContext Bug

Reasoning:
The previous task migrated the `resetAudioContext` logic to ES Modules, which detached it from the global `window` object. This caused the UI drift-correction interval to crash with a `TypeError`. Additionally, the Calibration Signal checkbox event listener was inadvertently omitted from `src/main.ts` due to a patching failure.

Changes:
- Removed `(window as any)` from `resetAudioContext` calls and updated module imports.
- Added `#testSignalToggle` event listener directly to `src/main.ts` to properly trigger `toggleCalibrationMode`.
```

### đź“– Walkthrough / Specs Checklist
# Fix Calibration Toggle and resetAudioContext Bug

## Acceptance Criteria Coverage
- Removed the `(window as any)` wrapper around `resetAudioContext` in `src/main.ts` and explicitly imported it from `src/audio.ts` to prevent undefined reference crashes on the 10-minute flush interval.
- Bound the `#testSignalToggle` checkbox to `toggleCalibrationMode` in `src/main.ts`, correctly initializing the Calibration Signal when clicked from the UI.

## Verification
- Code successfully builds via `tsc`.
- The Calibration Signal UI element acts as expected to spawn the `OscillatorNode` logic and the application no longer halts upon intervals.
